problem.title: Alien Dictionary
problem.description: Given a sorted dictionary (list of words) from an alien language, find the order of characters in that language. The words are sorted lexicographically according to the alien language's rules. Return any valid order, or an empty string if the input is invalid (e.g., cycle or contradiction).

examples.explanation: From 'wrt' and 'wrf', we get t < f. From 'wrf' and 'er', w < e. From 'er' and 'ett', r < t. From 'ett' and 'rftt', e < r. So order: w < e < r < t < f.

constraints: All characters are lowercase English letters
Words are sorted lexicographically in alien language
Return empty string if no valid order exists (cycle or contradiction)

edge_cases.single_word: Only one word — return all unique characters in any order
edge_cases.no_relations: All words start with different letters — only partial order

solution_approaches.name: Topological Sort with Edge Extraction

overall_complexity.time: O(C), where C = total characters in words

solution_approaches.intuition: Extract character precedence from adjacent word pairs. Build graph and run topological sort. If cycle exists or prefix violation, return empty string.

ladders.title: Full Alien Dictionary Solver
ladders.instruction: Given list of words, return valid character order or empty string if invalid.

ladders.title: Extract Character Relations from Adjacent Words
ladders.instruction: For each pair of adjacent words, find the first differing character to establish an edge.

ladders.title: Detect Prefix Violation
ladders.instruction: If word1 is longer than word2 and word2 is a prefix of word1, it's invalid.

test_cases.description: Invalid — prefix should come first (Additional case 6)

ladders.title: Build Graph of Character Dependencies
ladders.instruction: Use a map from char to set of chars that must come after it.

test_cases.expected_output: All appear in graph or as isolated nodes
All appear in graph or as isolated nodes

ladders.title: Run Topological Sort on Character Graph
ladders.instruction: Use Kahn’s algorithm to generate order. If not all chars are processed, return empty string.

test_cases.description: Cycle or disconnected with dependency (Additional case 10)

ladders.title: Why Topological Sort Is Correct
ladders.instruction: The alien dictionary defines a partial order; topological sort finds a linear extension if no cycles exist.

input.graph_theory: DAG has at least one topological order
DAG has at least one topological order

solution_approaches.name: Character Graph Construction
solution_approaches.intuition: Collect all unique characters and build directed edges based on lexicographical comparisons.

ladders.title: Collect All Unique Characters
ladders.instruction: Scan all words to gather the set of characters used.

ladders.title: Compare Adjacent Words to Find First Difference
ladders.instruction: For words[i] and words[i+1], iterate until characters differ.

ladders.title: Add Directed Edge for Character Order
ladders.instruction: From first differing chars c1 (in w1) and c2 (in w2), add edge c1 → c2.

ladders.title: Handle Identical Words
ladders.instruction: Skip if two adjacent words are identical — no information gained.

ladders.title: Ensure All Characters Are in Graph
ladders.instruction: Even isolated characters (no edges) must be included in the final order.

input.real_world: All letters must appear in alphabet
All letters must appear in alphabet

ladders.title: Time Complexity of Graph Construction
ladders.instruction: Total work is proportional to total characters in input.

solution_approaches.name: In-Degree Tracking and Kahn’s Algorithm
solution_approaches.intuition: Use Kahn’s algorithm on the character graph to generate topological order.

ladders.title: Compute In-Degree for Each Character
ladders.instruction: For each edge u→v, increment in_degree[v].

ladders.title: Initialize Queue with Zero In-Degree Characters
ladders.instruction: Add all chars with in_degree 0 to BFS queue.

test_cases.expected_output: Any zero in-degree order is valid
Any zero in-degree order is valid

ladders.title: Process Queue and Build Result String
ladders.instruction: Dequeue char, append to result, reduce in-degree of neighbors, enqueue if becomes 0.

test_cases.expected_output: After processing a: in_degree['c']=1; after b: in_degree['c']=0 → enqueue c
After processing a: in_degree['c']=1; after b: in_degree['c']=0 → enqueue c

ladders.title: Check if All Characters Were Processed
ladders.instruction: If result length < total unique chars, return empty string (cycle exists).

ladders.title: Why Kahn’s Algorithm Works for This Problem
ladders.instruction: Kahn’s algorithm detects cycles and produces a topological order if one exists.

input.compared_to_DFS: Kahn’s is iterative and easier to debug
Kahn’s is iterative and easier to debug

ladders.title: Handle Disconnected Components in Graph
ladders.instruction: Isolated characters (no edges) are added when their in-degree (0) is processed.

test_cases.description: But problem uses only given chars
But problem uses only given chars (Additional case 9)

solution_approaches.name: Lexicographical Order Analysis
solution_approaches.intuition: Lexicographical order between two words is determined by the first differing character.

ladders.title: Understand Lexicographical Comparison
ladders.instruction: In sorted list, for any i < j, words[i] ≤ words[j] in alien order.

test_cases.expected_output: Invalid — abc cannot come before ab
z < x → z comes before x
Invalid — abc cannot come before ab
z < x → z comes before x

ladders.title: Extract Only One Relation Per Word Pair
ladders.instruction: Only the first differing character gives a valid relation; subsequent chars are irrelevant.

test_cases.expected_output: Only t < f (ignore r,t vs r,f)
Only t < f (ignore r,t vs r,f)

ladders.title: Prefix Rule in Lexicographical Order
ladders.instruction: If word A is a prefix of word B, then A must come before B.

ladders.title: Why Adjacent Words Are Sufficient
ladders.instruction: Transitivity ensures that relations from non-adjacent words are implied by adjacent ones.

test_cases.expected_output: a < b and b < c implies a < c — no need for direct edge

input.real_world: Sorting algorithms only compare adjacent elements in some passes

input.mathematical_basis: Partial order is defined by its covering relations

test_cases.expected_output: a < b and b < c implies a < c — no need for direct edge

input.real_world: Sorting algorithms only compare adjacent elements in some passes

input.mathematical_basis: Partial order is defined by its covering relations

ladders.title: Handle Empty Word List
ladders.instruction: If no words, return empty string.

test_cases.description: Empty word — but constraints say len≥1

test_cases.expected_output: So not needed, but robust code handles it

test_cases.description: Empty word — but constraints say len≥1 (Additional case 7)

test_cases.expected_output: So not needed, but robust code handles it

ladders.title: Real-World Interpretation of Lexicographical Order
ladders.instruction: Alien dictionary models any custom sorting rule based on character precedence.

solution_approaches.name: Edge Case & Output Formatting
solution_approaches.intuition: Handle single word, prefix violations, cycles, and output format correctly.

ladders.title: Single Word Input
ladders.instruction: Return all unique characters in the word in any order.

test_cases.description: Any order like 'bca' also valid (Additional case 6)

ladders.title: Prefix Violation Detection
ladders.instruction: If a longer word comes before its prefix, return empty string.

ladders.title: Cycle Detection in Character Graph
ladders.instruction: If topological sort doesn't include all characters, a cycle exists — return empty string.

ladders.title: Output Is String of Characters or Empty
ladders.instruction: Return string of unique characters in valid order, or empty string if invalid.

ladders.title: Large Input Within Constraints
ladders.instruction: Handle up to 100 words of length 50 efficiently.

test_cases.expected_output: Valid order based on first char
Valid order based on first char

ladders.title: Real-World Applications
ladders.instruction: Alien dictionary models custom sorting rules in compilers, databases, and linguistics.

real_world_applications: Compiler design: determining operator or token precedence
Database systems: custom collation and sorting rules
Computational linguistics: inferring grammar rules from corpora
Bioinformatics: ordering genetic sequences with custom alphabets
Localization: supporting locale-specific character ordering (e.g., Spanish ñ)
Game development: creating consistent rules for fictional languages
